Instruction: You are a wine enthusiast (User ID: lisa_wong_456). Please ask the AI Service Agent to query the country of origin and taste profile of the wine you are pointing at that has a gold foil cap. Next, have the AI Service Agent check whether the bottle to the right of the wine with red liquid—which has a gold cap with red details—is on discount. If there is no discount, ask the AI Service Agent to find wines that share the same origin as the first bottle, have a "sweet" taste profile, and are priced below 300 yuan, and add the cheapest qualifying wine(s) to the shopping cart. analysis:
1. Query the country of origin and taste profile of the wine with a gold foil cap.
2. Check whether the wine to the right of the wine containing red liquid, which has a gold cap with red accents, is on discount.
3. If it is on discount, search for wines originating from France with a tax rate below 0.12 and a "low fat" characteristic. Add the wine with the lowest sugar content to the cart. If multiple wines tie for the lowest sugar content among those that meet the conditions, add all of them to the cart.
4. If it is not on discount, search for wines that share the same origin as the first bottle, have a "sweet" taste, and are priced below 300 yuan. Add the cheapest wine to the cart. If multiple wines tie for the lowest price among those that meet the conditions, add all of them to the cart.
5. Calculate the total tax for the items in the cart.

analysis:
1. Query the nutritional information and discount details of the first bottle of wine you are pointing at.

2. Check whether the bottle to the right of the wine with the pink label is produced in Italy.

3. If so, search for wines produced in Italy, with a tax rate below 0.12, and featuring "low sugar." Add the qualifying wine with the lowest calorie count to your cart. If multiple wines tie for the lowest calories, add one of each of those tied items to the cart.

4. If not, search for wines produced in France, featuring "low fat," and with a unit price below 200 yuan. Add the qualifying wine with the lowest price to your cart. If multiple wines tie for the lowest price, add one of each of those tied items to the cart.

5. Calculate the total calcium content of the items in the shopping cart.
